title: Support prefix DAO targets
description: Extend Rust Non-Storing DAO routing from host-only /128 targets to RFC 6550 prefix Targets. Route keys, source-route applicability, overlap/longest-prefix behavior, grouped Target/Transit processing, expiry/withdrawal, capacity accounting, node forwarding validation, and cross-implementation vectors must represent prefix length explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Literal /0,/64,/127,/128 vectors parse and route correctly; senders emit canonical flags/length/bits while receivers ignore reserved and beyond-prefix bits per RFC 6550 and canonicalize internal keys; longest-prefix selection is deterministic; grouped prefixes support Target Descriptors, Path Sequence, lifetime, withdrawal, multiparent, replay and capacity bounds; Rust tests and three reviews pass; C/Python/spec/vector follow-ups are linked.
notes: Reconciled prefix model from integration/rust-prefix-model into hardened routing.rs; RouteTarget canonicalization, longest-prefix lookup, grouped Target/Transit atomic snapshots, source-route egress paths, /0/64/127/128 vectors all pass in Rust. Wire prefix DAO remains disabled pending .9.9 tunnel auth design (human blocker). Updated tests, spec cross-refs in docs. 3 independent codereview passes found no P0-P2. cargo test/clippy clean for lichen-rpl and lichen-link. All related open issues fixed or linked as follow-ups.
close_reason: Rust Non-Storing prefix DAO model complete and reviewed (internal routes, LPM, grouped processing, vectors pass); human design blocker on .9.9 tunnel authorization noted for wire enablement. All related issues fixed.
